Differential Data Analysis

Uwe Schwartz

21/03/2024

Dependencies & Libraries

Load packages

library(DESeq2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(ggplot2)
library(pheatmap)
library(ggrepel)
library(tidyverse)
#palette(unique(c(brewer.pal(9,"Set1"),brewer.pal(8,"Set2"))))
load("/Users/admin///Library/Mobile Documents/com~apple~CloudDocs/Organisation_NAC/Cooperate_Design/colors/nac_palette_extend.rda")
palette(nac_palette_extend)

##Import data

# load 
# load 
load(file=paste(work.path,"obj/rld.rda",sep="/"))
load(file="obj/dds.obj.rda")
load(file="obj/tpm.mat.rda")

# meta data
colData(dds.obj)
## DataFrame with 35 rows and 3 columns
##                id genotype treatment
##       <character> <factor>  <factor>
## 48982       48982    H77L      PS341
## 47151       47151    C118F     DMSO 
## 47672       47672    C118F     DMSO 
## 46588       46588    Ctrl      PS341
## 47614       47614    C118F     PS341
## ...           ...      ...       ...
## 48975       48975    H77L      DMSO 
## 50091       50091    C118F     PS341
## 50095       50095    C118F     PS341
## 50093       50093    C118F     DMSO 
## 50096       50096    C118F     PS341
dds.obj$pairs<-factor(paste(dds.obj$genotype,dds.obj$treatment, sep="."))

#design
design(dds.obj)<-~pairs

## differential gene expression Wald test
dds.diff<-DESeq(dds.obj)
## estimating size factors
## estimating dispersions
## gene-wise dispersion estimates
## mean-dispersion relationship
## final dispersion estimates
## fitting model and testing
### get annotation of genes
biotype<-read.delim("/Users//admin//Annotation/MusMusculus/RNAseq_Challenge/all_genes_INFO.txt")

mx<-match(rownames(rowData(dds.diff)),biotype$ensembl_gene_id)
rowData(dds.diff)$biotype<-biotype$biotype[mx]
rowData(dds.diff)$symbol<-biotype$mgi_symbol[mx]
rowData(dds.diff)$EnsemblID<-biotype$ensembl_gene_id[mx]

table(rowData(dds.diff)$EnsemblID==rownames(rowData(dds.diff)))
## 
##  TRUE 
## 33292
table(is.na(rowData(dds.diff)$biotype))
## 
## FALSE 
## 33292
resultsNames(dds.diff)
## [1] "Intercept"                       "pairs_C118F.PS341_vs_C118F.DMSO"
## [3] "pairs_Ctrl.DMSO_vs_C118F.DMSO"   "pairs_Ctrl.PS341_vs_C118F.DMSO" 
## [5] "pairs_H77L.DMSO_vs_C118F.DMSO"   "pairs_H77L.PS341_vs_C118F.DMSO"

H77L.DMSO vs Ctrl.DMSO

#### differential analysis
ctrl="Ctrl.DMSO"
cond="H77L.DMSO"

res<-results(dds.diff, contrast=c("pairs",cond, ctrl))
summary(res, alpha=0.05)
## 
## out of 23943 with nonzero total read count
## adjusted p-value < 0.05
## LFC > 0 (up)       : 2524, 11%
## LFC < 0 (down)     : 2341, 9.8%
## outliers [1]       : 132, 0.55%
## low counts [2]     : 8565, 36%
## (mean count < 2)
## [1] see 'cooksCutoff' argument of ?results
## [2] see 'independentFiltering' argument of ?results
## pVal_distribution
hist(res$pvalue[res$baseMean > 1], breaks = 0:20/20,
       col = "grey50", border = "white", xlab="p-Value", main="pVal distribution")

#MA plot before shrinkage
max.lfc<-max(abs(res$log2FoldChange),na.rm = T)

DESeq2::plotMA(res,ylim = c(-max.lfc, max.lfc), cex=0.5,
               colSig=1, alpha=0.05)

#shrinkage
res.shr <- lfcShrink(dds.diff, 
                     contrast=c("pairs",cond, ctrl), 
                     res=res, type = "ashr") 
## using 'ashr' for LFC shrinkage. If used in published research, please cite:
##     Stephens, M. (2016) False discovery rates: a new deal. Biostatistics, 18:2.
##     https://doi.org/10.1093/biostatistics/kxw041
# get Gene annotation
res.pre<-cbind(res.shr,rowData(dds.diff)[rownames(res.shr),
                                    c("EnsemblID","biotype", "symbol")],
           tpm.mat[row.names(res.shr),order(colnames(tpm.mat))])

head(res.pre[order(res$padj),])
## DataFrame with 6 rows and 43 columns
##                     baseMean log2FoldChange     lfcSE       pvalue         padj
##                    <numeric>      <numeric> <numeric>    <numeric>    <numeric>
## ENSMUSG00000037820   3175.95        3.43351  0.125230 1.00542e-167 1.53287e-163
## ENSMUSG00000030774   9240.09       -2.67105  0.129738  1.24496e-95  9.49032e-92
## ENSMUSG00000046719   1567.13       -2.27467  0.130663  7.07240e-69  3.59419e-65
## ENSMUSG00000030409   1688.68        4.70442  0.273917  2.11935e-68  8.07789e-65
## ENSMUSG00000037664  19430.08       -4.42899  0.262269  2.28053e-66  6.95380e-63
## ENSMUSG00000041378   4582.24       -4.45695  0.264247  3.29255e-66  8.36636e-63
##                             EnsemblID        biotype      symbol TPM_46301
##                           <character>    <character> <character> <numeric>
## ENSMUSG00000037820 ENSMUSG00000037820 protein_coding        Tgm2  182.9754
## ENSMUSG00000030774 ENSMUSG00000030774 protein_coding        Pak1  173.1343

##                     TPM_47063 TPM_47067 TPM_47151  TPM_47155  TPM_47157

## ENSMUSG00000037820   18.41950  184.9172  145.4368   20.14278   15.38855

## ENSMUSG00000037664 4710.25293  475.1580  281.4545 4503.21518 4804.22392
## ENSMUSG00000041378 1678.97656  134.0168   63.9343 1997.42387 1642.07131
##                     TPM_47221 TPM_47222 TPM_47592 TPM_47614 TPM_47615 TPM_47672
##                     <numeric> <numeric> <numeric> <numeric> <numeric> <numeric>
## ENSMUSG00000037820   15.96158  168.4143  195.2520  162.4133  198.0073  217.4483

##                    TPM_47991 TPM_47993 TPM_48638 TPM_48813 TPM_48814 TPM_48975
##                    <numeric> <numeric> <numeric> <numeric> <numeric> <numeric>
## ENSMUSG00000037820  221.3276  179.3567  172.3318  196.9814  191.2360  203.9236
## ENSMUSG00000030774  200.2015  225.4879  263.5884  173.3222  183.0799  124.2262
## ENSMUSG00000046719   55.0711   72.4694   95.9501   63.0661   64.6245   67.7394
## ENSMUSG00000030409  115.8328  122.1741   74.0872  109.6255  104.0172  118.3474
## ENSMUSG00000037664  438.2137  556.6229  458.9053  277.4822  285.1122   76.4057
## ENSMUSG00000041378  126.5022  148.7838  161.7638   77.7170   99.2517   24.9832
##                    TPM_48982  TPM_48986 TPM_49335 TPM_50091 TPM_50093 TPM_50095
##                    <numeric>  <numeric> <numeric> <numeric> <numeric> <numeric>
## ENSMUSG00000037820  188.9300   20.19766  228.7619  234.7390  195.0892  172.7825
## ENSMUSG00000030774  197.6520 1060.18419  148.3415  211.4823  182.3530  182.4294
## ENSMUSG00000046719   64.8332  265.33302   58.8376   80.2040   75.3351   56.1589
## ENSMUSG00000030409  127.3031    1.62315   39.5698  214.4429   72.6720  265.7630
## ENSMUSG00000037664  300.0452 4156.33547  162.1716  154.3255  146.3464  139.1160
## ENSMUSG00000041378   89.0223 1232.28668   53.1651   52.4079   49.3209   43.5581
##                    TPM_50096
##                    <numeric>
## ENSMUSG00000037820  224.0829
## ENSMUSG00000030774  169.1427
## ENSMUSG00000046719   61.6410
## ENSMUSG00000030409  137.6601
## ENSMUSG00000037664  145.9624
## ENSMUSG00000041378   68.7654
###### filter for TPM>1 in at least all grouped samples (used)

cond.samps<-colData(dds.diff) %>% 
    as.tibble() %>% 
    filter(pairs %in% cond) %>% 
    pull(id)

ctrl.samps<-colData(dds.diff) %>% 
    as.tibble() %>% 
    filter(pairs %in% ctrl) %>% 
    pull(id)


cond.pos<-res.pre %>% as.tibble() %>% 
    select(contains(cond.samps)) %>% 
    apply(1,function(x) sum(x>1)==length(cond.samps))

ctrl.pos<-res.pre %>% as.tibble() %>% 
    select(contains(ctrl.samps)) %>% 
    apply(1,function(x) sum(x>1)==length(ctrl.samps))

    
res<-res.pre[(ctrl.pos | cond.pos),]


## set thresholds
p.th<-0.01
logFC<-1

### significant hits
res.sig<-subset(res, padj<p.th & abs(log2FoldChange) > logFC)
#significant hits up regulated
table(res.sig$log2FoldChange>0)
## 
## FALSE  TRUE 
##   269   668
################################################################################
##########################  MA Plot     ########################################
################################################################################
df<-data.frame(subset(res, !is.na(res$padj)))

df$signif<-df$padj<=p.th & abs(df$log2FoldChange)>logFC

df$colorBI<-"ns"
df$colorBI[df$signif & df$log2FoldChange>0]<-"up"
df$colorBI[df$signif & df$log2FoldChange<0]<-"down"

df$colorBI<-factor(df$colorBI,levels = c("ns","down","up"))

n.group<-table(df$colorBI)
###########################################################

g.MA<-ggplot(df, aes(log10(baseMean),log2FoldChange))+
    geom_point(aes(color=colorBI))+
    theme_bw()+
    xlab("log10(normalized counts)")+
    scale_color_manual(values=c("grey90","#1F78B4","#E31A1C"),
                       labels=c(paste("ns",n.group["ns"], sep="="),
                                paste("down",n.group["down"], sep="="),
                                paste("up",n.group["up"], sep="=")))+
    geom_point(data=subset(df,df$colorBI=="up"),
               aes(log10(baseMean),log2FoldChange), color="#E31A1C")+
    geom_point(data=subset(df,df$colorBI=="down"),
               aes(log10(baseMean),log2FoldChange), color="#1F78B4")+
    theme(legend.position = "top", 
          legend.text = element_text(size=15),
          )+
    guides(colour = guide_legend("",override.aes = list(size = 3)), 
          )+
    geom_hline(yintercept = c(logFC,-logFC),  linetype="dashed")+
    geom_hline(yintercept =0, color= "grey20",alpha = 0.3, size=1.5  )+
    ggtitle(paste0("FDR = ",p.th ," & |log2FC| > ",logFC))

g.MA

## heatmap of significant genes 
mat<-assay(rld)[rownames(res.sig),]
rownames(mat)<- res.sig$symbol
anno<-as.data.frame(colData(rld)[,c("genotype", "treatment")])

colData(rld)
## DataFrame with 35 rows and 4 columns
##                id genotype treatment sizeFactor
##       <character> <factor>  <factor>  <numeric>
## 48982       48982    H77L      PS341   1.053798
## 47151       47151    C118F     DMSO    0.987088
## 47672       47672    C118F     DMSO    0.991417
## 46588       46588    Ctrl      PS341   0.946305
## 47614       47614    C118F     PS341   1.132250
## ...           ...      ...       ...        ...
## 48975       48975    H77L      DMSO    1.154622
## 50091       50091    C118F     PS341   0.768007
## 50095       50095    C118F     PS341   1.321448
## 50093       50093    C118F     DMSO    1.037928
## 50096       50096    C118F     PS341   1.243171
ordCol<-colData(rld)[order(rld$genotype,rld$treatment),"id"]
idx<-colData(rld)[ordCol,"genotype"]=="C118F"


#tested samples
print(pheatmap(mat[,c(ctrl.samps,cond.samps)],scale="row", 
               annotation_col = anno,show_rownames  = F,
               cluster_cols = F,breaks = seq(-2,2,length.out= 101),
               color = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(n = 7, name ="RdBu")))(100))
      ) 

#all samples  
print(pheatmap(mat[,c(ordCol[!idx],ordCol[idx])],scale="row", 
               annotation_col = anno,show_rownames  = F,
               cluster_cols = F,breaks = seq(-2,2,length.out= 101),
               color = colorRampPalette(rev(brewer.pal(n = 7, name ="RdBu")))(100))
      ) 

################################################################################
########################## volcano plot ########################################
################################################################################

df<-data.frame(subset(res, !is.na(res$padj)))

df$signif<-df$padj<=p.th & abs(df$log2FoldChange)>logFC

df$colorBI<-"ns"
df$colorBI[df$signif & df$log2FoldChange>0]<-"up"
df$colorBI[df$signif & df$log2FoldChange<0]<-"down"

df$colorBI<-factor(df$colorBI,levels = c("ns","down","up"))

n.group<-table(df$colorBI)
#################################################################

g.volc<-ggplot(df, aes(log2FoldChange,log10(padj)*c(-1)))+
    geom_point(aes(color=colorBI))+theme_bw()+ylab("-log10(p.adj)")+
    scale_color_manual(values=c("grey90","#1F78B4","#E31A1C"),
                       labels=c(paste("ns",n.group["ns"], sep="="),
                                paste("down",n.group["down"], sep="="),
                                paste("up",n.group["up"], sep="=")))+
    geom_point(data=subset(df,df$colorBI=="up"),
               aes(log2FoldChange,log10(padj)*c(-1)), color="#E31A1C")+
    geom_point(data=subset(df,df$colorBI=="down"),
               aes(log2FoldChange,log10(padj)*c(-1)), color="#1F78B4")+
    guides(colour = guide_legend("",override.aes = list(size = 3)))+ 
    theme(legend.position = "top",
          legend.text = element_text(size=15))+
    geom_segment(aes(x = logFC, y = -log10(p.th), xend=Inf, yend=-log10(p.th)),  linetype="dashed")+
    geom_segment(aes(x =-logFC, y =-log10(p.th), xend=-Inf, yend=-log10(p.th)),  linetype="dashed")+
    geom_segment(aes(x = logFC, y = -log10(p.th), xend=1, yend=Inf),  linetype="dashed")+
    geom_segment(aes(x =-logFC, y =-log10(p.th), xend=-1, yend=Inf),  linetype="dashed")+
#   geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(p.th),  linetype="dashed")+
    ggtitle(paste0("FDR = ",p.th ," & |log2FC| > ",logFC))
g.volc    

############################
#get top 20 p-adjusted value
df.sign<-subset(df, signif)
df.top<-df.sign[order(df.sign$padj)[1:20],]


print(g.volc+geom_text_repel(data = df.top,
                        aes(label = symbol),
                        size = 3,max.overlaps = NA,
                        box.padding = unit(0.3, "lines"),
                        point.padding = unit(0.2, "lines")))
